revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ichinomiya asama-jinja]][[en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]][[ja:浅間神社 (笛吹市)]][[zh:淺間神社 (笛吹市)]] {{Expand Japanese|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki) | native_name = 浅間神社 | image = Asama-jinja (Fuefuki), haiden-1.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki) | coordinates = {{coord|35.647758333333|138.69744166667}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|lt=Konohananosakuya-bime|az|Konohanasakuya|ca|Konohana no Sakuya hime|de|Konohanasakuyahime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|fa|کونوهاناساکویا-هیمه|fr|Konohana-no-Sakuya|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|ko|고노하나노사쿠야비메|mni|ꯀꯣꯅꯣꯍꯅꯁꯀꯨꯌ-ꯍꯤꯃꯦ|pl|Konohanasakuya-hime|ru|Коноханасакуя-химэ|sv|Konohanasakuyahime|th|เซ็นเง็ง|vi|Konohana Sakuya Hime|zh|木花開耶姬|qid=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | website = {{Official website|https://asamajinja.jp}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ichinomiya Asama Shrine'''|浅間神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kai Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|fa|زیارتگاه سنگن|fr|Sanctuaires Asama|it|Santuario di Asama|ja|浅間神社|sv|Asama-helgedom|zh|淺間神社|qid=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya|lt=ichinomiya|ca|Ichinomiya (Xinto)|de|Ichi-no-miya|en|Ichinomiya|fr|Ichi-no-miya|id|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|ko|이치노미야|pt|Ichinomiya (templo)|simple|Ichinomiya|th|อิจิโนมิยะ|uk|Ітіномія (титул)|zh|一宮|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} ** P1001: {{ill|Q858076|ltq=Q858076|qid=Q858076|1=Kai Province}} * {{ill|Q10898274|ltq=Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} * shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * == * {{coord|35.647758333333|138.69744166667}} == official website (P856) == * https://asamajinja.jp ** Language of work: {{ill|Q5287|ltq=Q5287|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * https://web.archive.org/web/20060723061528/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/146001.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181673 ** P3831: Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * Sengen Shrine|lt=Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|en|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社|qid=Q11352589|1=Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine}} == Instagram username (P2003) == * asamajinja == Facebook username (P2013) == Ichinomiya asama-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039708|ltq=Q135039708|qid=Q135039708|1=Asamano Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山梨県笛吹市一宮町一ノ宮1684', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '1684 Ichinomiya-chō Ichinomiya, Fuefuki-shi, Yamanashi-ken 405-0056', 'language': 'en'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Asama-jinja (Fuefuki), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Ichinomiya asama-jinja}} [[EN:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]] [[JA:浅間神社 (笛吹市)]] [[ZH:淺間神社 (笛吹市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11557476}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Asama Shrine |Image = [[File:Asama-jinja (Fuefuki), haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) (designated cultural property of Fuefuki City) |Location = {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|qid=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fuefuki City|Q849434|qid=Q849434|1=Fuefuki}} Ichinomiya-chō Ichinomiya 1684 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|35|38|51.93|N|138|41|50.79|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |Shintai = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsya|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
{{ill|lt=Kai Province|Q858076|qid=Q858076|1=Kai Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokuhei Chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |Founded = (Trad.) 8th year of the 11th Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = Ichinomiya Asama Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}} |Rituals = Omiyuki Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=51.93|lon_deg=138|lon_min=41|lon_sec=50.79|label=Asama Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|51.93|N|138|41|50.79|E}}}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Asama-jinja (Fuefuki), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Second torii gate}}]] '''Asama Shrine''' (Asama-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ichinomiya-chō Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Fuefuki City|Q849434|qid=Q849434|1=Fuefuki}}, {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|qid=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsya|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Kai Province|Q858076|qid=Q858076|1=Kai Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokuhei Chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is currently a {{ill|lt=Beppyō Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|qid=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is one of the many {{ill|lt=Asama Shrines|Q3471785|qid=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} found throughout Japan. Because it is the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Kai Province|Q858076|qid=Q858076|1=Kai Province}}, it is commonly known as “Ichinomiya Asama Shrine” and is also affectionately called “Ichinomiya-san.” === Enshrined deity === The shrine currently enshrines the following single deity: * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} (konohanasakuya-hime-no-mikoto) *: A deity who personifies Mount Fuji. She was originally one of three deities enshrined at Yamamiya Shrine, and it is said that only Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto was transferred here. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the first month of the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} at the foot of the sacred mountain (present-day Sessha Yamamiya Shrine). It is said to have been transferred to its current location on the 9th day of the 12th month (lunar calendar) of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|qid=Q30350|1=865}}). The surrounding area was the center of ancient {{ill|lt=Kai Province|Q858076|qid=Q858076|1=Kai Province}}, and nearby remain the ruins of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kai Provincial Temple|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Kai Provincial Convent|Q131909627|qid=Q131909627|1=Kai Kokubun-niji}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of the candidate shrines (ronsya) for “Asama Shrine of Yatsushiro District, Kai Province,” which was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is also said to have been regarded as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province from the late Heian period. However, because the shrine is located in the former {{ill|lt=Yamanashi District|Q11469930|qid=Q11469930|1=Yamanashi District}} and because there are other shrines that also claim to be the Ichinomiya of Kai Province, there are theories that this shrine was not actually the Myōjin Taisha or the Ichinomiya. According to the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, following the {{ill|lt=Jōgan eruption|Q11634546|qid=Q11634546|1=Jōgan eruption of Mount Fuji}} of {{ill|lt=Mount Fuji|Q39231|qid=Q39231|1=Mount Fuji}} in {{ill|lt=864|Q25041|qid=Q25041|1=864}}, Asama deities were enshrined in Kai Province, and in the following year, Jōgan 7 (865), on the 9th day of the 12th lunar month, Asama Shrine was built in Yatsushiro District and designated as a government shrine. It also records that another Asama Shrine was later built in Yamanashi District. For this reason, the prevailing theory is that this shrine was “the Asama Shrine later built in Yamanashi District,” though it is also possible that at the time of its founding, the area belonged to Yatsushiro District and that it was “the first Asama Shrine built in Yatsushiro District” (see “{{ill|lt=Asama Shrine#Kai Province|Q3471785|qid=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}}” for details). Another view holds that the subsidiary Yamamiya Shrine was originally a shrine dedicated to the sacred mountain itself, and that as development of the Kōfu Basin progressed, the shrine moved to the village (satomiya) and was later endowed with the divine character of the Asama deity (Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto)“Japanese Deities,” entry on Ichinomiya Asama Shrine.. The shrine enjoyed deep reverence from the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|qid=Q1210114|1=Takeda clan}}, and many related documents have been handed down. From that period onward, historical sources referring to this shrine as the Ichinomiya and documents mentioning “Ichinomiya Estate” in the area can be found“Colophon of the Daihannyakyō held at Chōshō-ji,” copied in the latter half of April of {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1496|Q6699|qid=Q6699|1=1496}}), among others (from “Place Names of Yamanashi Prefecture”)., and it was generally revered as the Ichinomiya of Kai. Even after entering the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it continued to be protected, for example by confirmations of its landholdings from the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|qid=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}}. On the 14th day of the 5th month (lunar calendar) of {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|qid=Q7746|1=1871}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokuhei Chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and after the war it became a {{ill|lt=Beppyō Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}}. In {{ill|lt=Shinto|Q812767|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}}, {{ill|lt=sake|Q170219|qid=Q170219|1=Sake}} is offered to the deities as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=omiki|1=UNKNOWN}} (sacred sake), but at Ichinomiya Asama Shrine, since {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|qid=Q2650|1=1965}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 40), {{ill|lt=wine|Q282|qid=Q282|1=Wine}} produced in Yamanashi Prefecture has been offered as omiki“Grapes and Wine” (Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2016), p.110. === Grounds === The precincts cover a little over 1 hectare. The worship hall stands behind pine trees and faces at a right angle to the approach. The worship hall was built in {{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|qid=Q7614|1=1672}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12) during the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. It is a single-story {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} structure with a karahafu-style porch, measuring 7 bays by 3 bays, with a copper-sheeted roof. It is designated as a cultural property of Fuefuki City. To the left inside the sacred fence of the main hall grows a pair of “married plum trees.” This rare variety bears two fruits from a single flower (fused at the sides) and is designated a natural monument of Yamanashi Prefecture. Although this shrine enshrines Mount Fuji, the mountain cannot be seen from the grounds as it is hidden by the {{ill|lt=Misaka Mountains|Q11488873|qid=Q11488873|1=Misaka Mountains}}, and the main hall does not face in the direction of Mount Fuji. Shrine buildings and pine trees|Precincts File:Asama-jinja (Fuefuki), honden.jpg|Main hall and married plum trees (Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monument) File:Asama-jinja (Fuefuki), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Asama-jinja (Fuefuki), shinmon.jpg|Zuishin Gate File:Asama-jinja (Fuefuki), komochiishi.jpg|Komochi-ishi (Child-bearing stone) File:Ichinomiya asama-jinja 1st Gate.jpg|First torii gate === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === === Subsidiary shrine === [[File:YamamiyaP9130064.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Subsidiary Yamamiya Shrine main hall (National {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}})}}]] * Yamamiya Shrine (subsidiary shrine outside the grounds) ** Location: Ichinomiya-chō Ichinomiya, Fuefuki City (enclave; {{Wiki coordinates|35|38|02.31|N|138|42|50.33|E|region:JP|location|name=Yamamiya Shrine (subsidiary shrine)}}) ** Enshrined deities: {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q1056122|qid=Q1056122|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|qid=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} – the father and husband deities of Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto *: Located south of the main shrine at the foot of the sacred mountain, this is the original site of Asama Shrine and corresponds to the former shrine (motomiya). Worship was conducted here from its founding in the 8th year of Emperor Suinin until the transfer on the 9th day of the 12th lunar month of Jōgan 7 (865), at which time only Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto was transferred to the village shrine (the present main shrine)“Kai Province Ichinomiya Asama Shrine Abridged History.”. Since ancient times, the Yamamiya Shinkō Festival has been held on {{ill|lt=March 15|Q2403|qid=Q2403|1=March 15}}, during which a portable shrine is carried from the main shrine (now held on a Sunday around the 15th). The shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|qid=Q6601|1=1558}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 1) and is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. === Auxiliary shrines === * Shrines within the grounds ** Gokoku Shrine ** Seven Shrines – Enshrined deities: Amefuri Ōkami, Dōsojin, Inari Ōkami, Kotohira Ōkami, Rokusho Ōkami, Kagutsuchi Ōkami, Tenman-gū ** Shinmei Shrine ** Masada Shrine – Enshrined deity: Ban Masada (who delivered an oracle at the time of the shrine’s founding and later became a priest) * Shrines outside the grounds ** Tenjin Shrine File:Asama-jinja (Fuefuki), Gokokusha.jpg|Gokoku Shrine File:浅間神社 (笛吹市) 七社.JPG|Seven Shrines File:Asama-jinja (Fuefuki), Shinmeisha.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:浅間神社 (笛吹市) 境外末社天神社.JPG|Auxiliary Tenjin Shrine outside the grounds === Festivals === === Annual festivals ===
Asama Shrine Annual Festival List
* Monthly ** Monthly festival at the main shrine (1st) ** Monthly festival at Yamamiya Shrine (15th) * January ** New Year’s Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|qid=Q2150|1=January 1}}) ** Genshi Festival ({{ill|lt=January 3|Q2156|qid=Q2156|1=January 3}}) * February ** Tsuina Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|qid=Q2315|1=February 3}}) ** National Foundation Day Festival ({{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|qid=Q2335|1=February 11}}) ** Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|qid=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * March ** Yamamiya Shinkō Festival (Sunday before {{ill|lt=March 15|Q2403|qid=Q2403|1=March 15}}) ** Kōsha Festival * April ** Grand Annual Festival ({{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}}) ** Ōmiyuki Festival (April 15) ** Plum Branch Offering Ritual (second Day of the Boar in the 4th lunar month) * June ** Rice-planting Festival ({{ill|lt=June 17|Q2672|qid=Q2672|1=June 17}}) ** Summer Great Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|qid=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * October ** Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|qid=Q2962|1=October 17}}) * November ** Shichi-Go-San Festival ({{ill|lt=November 15|Q2982|qid=Q2982|1=November 15}}) ** Niiname Festival (Thanksgiving for the New Harvest) ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|qid=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * December ** Year-end Great Purification and New Year’s Eve Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|qid=Q2912|1=December 31}})
=== Ōmiyuki Festival === The Ōmiyuki Festival is held after the Grand Annual Festival on April 15 and is commonly called “Omiyuki-san.” In ancient times it was considered the greatest festival in Kai Province. For details, see “{{ill|lt=Shingen Embankment#Ryūō Kawarajuku and the Omiyuki Festival|Q4420072|qid=Q4420072|1=Shingen Embankment#Ryūō Kawarajuku and the Omiyuki Festival}}.” It began as a river-pacification (flood prevention) ritual due to the frequent great floods that struck Kai Province, during which the portable shrine is carried to Ryūō Sansha Shrine (on the {{ill|lt=Shingen Embankment|Q4420072|qid=Q4420072|1=Shingen Embankment}}). In the past, the procession went to Ryūō Sansha Shrine on the second Day of the Boar in April (summer procession) and to Ishida Sansha Shrine on the second Day of the Boar in November (winter procession). Along with this shrine as the Ichinomiya, the Ninomiya {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|Q11608850|qid=Q11608850|lt=Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)}} and the Sannomiya {{ill|lt=Tamamoro Shrine (Kōfu City)|Q11572792|qid=Q11572792|lt=Tamamoro Shrine|1=Tamamoro Shrine}} participate (from the Meiji period until recent years, only this shrine participated). According to shrine tradition, the festival has been held since {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|qid=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=825|Q24624|qid=Q24624|1=825}}), but in reality it is thought to have begun sometime after the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|qid=Q6601|1=1558}}–{{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|qid=Q6659|1=1570}}). === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national designation) === * Heart Sutra on indigo paper in gold ink (Konshi kindei Hannya Shingyō) (calligraphy) *: A {{ill|lt=Heart Sutra|Q622868|qid=Q622868|1=Heart Sutra (interlinear translation)}} that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|qid=Q349467|1=Emperor Go-Nara}} planned to dedicate to the Ichinomiya of various provinces in {{ill|lt=1541|Q6516|qid=Q6516|1=1541}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun|Q1192853|qid=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 10) during the Sengoku period to pray for peace. Actual dedications were made in 24 provinces, and the sutra survives today in 7 provinces including Kai. According to an ink inscription on the wrapping paper, this sutra was brought to Kai in Tenbun 19 ({{ill|lt=1550|Q6557|qid=Q6557|1=1550}}) by {{ill|lt=Dōchō|Q7137321|qid=Q7137321|1=Dōchō}} of the Shōgoin Monzeki, and was dedicated to the Ichinomiya by the Kai provincial governor {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|qid=Q276404|lt=Takeda Harunobu|1=Takeda Shingen}} (Shingen). Designated on April 4, {{ill|lt=1905|Q2047|qid=Q2047|1=1905}} (Meiji 38). * Main hall of the subsidiary Yamamiya Shrine, with four ridge tags attached (structure) *: Located at the former shrine site (enclave). Designated on August 28, 1907 (Meiji 40). === Cultural properties designated by Yamanashi Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Tachi sword, inscription Kunitsugu (craft) – said to have been dedicated by Takeda Shingen. Designated May 13, 1965 (Shōwa 40). ** Tachi sword, inscription Ittokusai Sukenori – designated January 27, 1972 (Shōwa 47). * Natural monuments ** Married plum trees of Ichinomiya Asama Shrine – designated November 7, 1960 (Shōwa 35). === Cultural properties designated by Fuefuki City === * Tangible cultural properties ** Worship hall, with one old material attached (structure) ** Wakatan poetry slips of Lord Takeda Shingen (calligraphy) * Natural monuments ** Married cedar trees of Yamamiya Shrine === Visitor information === '''Address''' * Ichinomiya-chō Ichinomiya 1684, {{ill|lt=Fuefuki City|Q849434|qid=Q849434|1=Fuefuki}}, {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|qid=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}} '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Chūō Main Line|Q1078110|qid=Q1078110|1=Chūō Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Yamanashishi Station|Q8013369|qid=Q8013369|1=Yamanashishi Station}} (about 60 minutes on foot or about 10 minutes by taxi) * Highway bus: From {{ill|lt=Busta Shinjuku|Q22123549|qid=Q22123549|1=Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal}}, take the {{ill|lt=Chūō Express Bus|Q5119039|qid=Q5119039|1=Chūō Kōsoku Bus}} and get off at the “Ichinomiya” bus stop (10-minute walk) * By car ** 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Katsunuma Interchange|Q10901397|qid=Q10901397|lt=Katsunuma IC|1=Katsunuma interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Expressway|Q1091276|qid=Q1091276|1=Chūō Expressway}}, turn right at the “Ishi” traffic light ** From the Kōfu area, turn left at “Ichinomiya Asama Shrine Entrance” '''Nearby''' * {{ill|lt=Kaina Shrine (Ichinomiya-chō Hashidate, Fuefuki City)|Q11577877|qid=Q11577877|lt=Kaina Shrine|1=Kaina Shrine}} – believed to be the provincial {{ill|lt=Sōsha|Q1107129|qid=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} of Kai Province. * Ruins of Kai Provincial Temple * Ruins of Kai Provincial Convent === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * “Kai Province Ichinomiya Asama Shrine Abridged History” (shrine history pamphlet) * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Yamanashi Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Asama Shrine, Ichinomiya-chō, Higashiyatsushiro District * Norio Saitō, “Ichinomiya Asama Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Japanese Deities – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 10 Tōkai” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}) * Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.), “Report on the Investigation of Shikinaisha, Vol. 10” (Kōgakkan University Press), entry on Asama Shrine === External links === {{commonscat|Ichinomiya asama-jinja}} * [http://asamajinja.jp/ Kai Province Ichinomiya Asama Shrine] – official website * {{Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection|146001}} {{Shinto navigation}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha
